data.description:
## Reproduction Steps
1. Run the graph tests that call `add_dependency` with pytest's basetemp nested inside a git repo (e.g. `TMPDIR=$PWD/.pytest-tmp`).
2. `add_dependency` -> `_write_link_event` runs `git -C <tracker> add/commit` against a tracker dir that is NOT its own git repo.
3. Git walks UP and commits the test's LINK events into the enclosing checkout.

Expected: test git writes are fully contained in the disposable tmp tracker.
Actual: dozens of `ticket: link ...` commits (and `.pytest-tmp/**` files) leaked onto the rebar repo's branch.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] An autouse fixture in tests/scripts/test_ticket_graph.py pins GIT_CEILING_DIRECTORIES so git cannot escape the tmp tree into any enclosing repo
- [ ] Nested-tmp reproduction creates zero commits on the surrounding repo
- [ ] All test_ticket_graph.py tests (incl. real-push/sync tests) still pass in a normal tmp
data.title: test_ticket_graph git writes can escape into the enclosing repo
